title: Structures, Unions, Tagged Variants, and Flexible Arrays · Independent C Trial
summary: An independent maintainer ports the tagged packet representation to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of tagged packet representation with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Initialize, copy, compare, and validate structures by members and invariants rather than raw-byte assumptions.
Pair a union with an explicit discriminant and access only the active, validated member.
Allocate flexible-array objects with checked header-plus-elements arithmetic and record their element count.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Aggregate Invariants
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 2419. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a tagged payload and its allocation size; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
